hero.title: Prevent clinical information loss between shifts
hero.subtitle: Centralize SBAR handoffs, vital signs, treatments, and crinical events in one secure, clear, and real-time accessible platform.
problem.title: The problem we solve
problem.desc: In many clinical environments, patient information is still scattered across paper notes, chats, partial records, or manual processes. This can lead to omissions, delays, and loss of continuity of care. PulseReport centralizes clinical communication and event traceability to help healthcare staff work with more order, clarity, and safety.
audience.title: Who is it for?
audience.1.title: Hospitals
audience.1.desc: Institutions that need to improve continuity of care and clinical traceability across departments and shifts.
audience.2.title: Private clinics
audience.2.desc: Centers looking to digitize critical clinical processes and better organize patient information.
audience.3.title: Cardiovascular nursing areas
audience.3.desc: Teams that need to record SBAR, vital signs, treatments, and clinical events with greater order and speed.
steps.title: How it works in 3 steps
steps.1.title: Record clinical information
steps.1.desc: Register SBAR handoffs, vital signs, treatments, and clinical events in one single platform.
steps.2.title: Share and monitor
steps.2.desc: Access updated information in real time to support continuity of care across shifts and departments.
steps.3.title: Trace and audit
steps.3.desc: Keep a clear history of actions, events, and decisions to improve control, follow-up, and safety.
features.title: Key features

benefits.3: Improves traceability of important events and actions.
benefits.4: Reduces dependence on scattered or manual records.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers before booking a demo.
faq.1.q: Is PulseReport only focused on cardiovascular nursing?
faq.1.a: It is especially designed for that environment, but its structure can be adapted to other specialized clinical contexts.
faq.2.q: Can the information be accessed in real time?
faq.2.a: Yes, the platform is designed to provide fast and updated access to the main clinical records.
faq.3.q: Is it a solution for hospitals and clinics?
faq.3.a: Yes, it is aimed at hospitals, private clinics, specialized centers, and healthcare professionals who need order and traceability.
faq.4.q: What service model does PulseReport offer?
faq.4.a: PulseReport proposes a subscription-based SaaS model, with the ability to scale according to each institution’s needs.
faq.5.q: Does PulseReport integrate with other hospital systems?
faq.5.a: PulseReport is designed to fit alongside existing hospital systems, and our team reviews each institution's integration needs case by case.
faq.6.q: What kind of support does the team provide?
faq.6.a: Our team offers onboarding guidance and ongoing support to help clinical staff adopt the platform smoothly.
faq.7.q: How is access set up for the clinical team?
faq.7.a: Each institution defines roles and permissions, so every team member only accesses the information relevant to their work.
testimonials.title: What people say about PulseReport
testimonials.lead: Clinical teams that have adopted PulseReport share their experience with the platform.
testimonials.1.text: "PulseReport transformed our shift handoffs. We used to lose up to 20 minutes searching for scattered notes; now the incoming team has everything on screen before we finish the briefing."
testimonials.1.name: Gloria Carbajal
testimonials.2.text: "The critical event traceability helped us enormously during an internal audit. We had the complete history ready in minutes — something that previously took us days to reconstruct."
testimonials.2.name: Carlos Ramírez
testimonials.3.text: "My nurses adopted the platform in less than a week. The interface is clear, not overwhelming, and fits the real pace of a cardiovascular ward."
testimonials.3.name: Lucía Portocarrero
testimonials.4.text: "Recording vital signs and treatments in one place eliminated the duplicates we had before. Shift continuity improved noticeably from the very first month."
testimonials.4.name: Marcos Arrieta

footer.desc: Digital solutions to optimize critical clinical processes, strengthen traceability, and improve continuity of care.
problem.lead: Clinical environments handle critical, time-sensitive information every day at every hour and yet the tools used to manage that information often fall short.
problem.card.1.title: Manual, paper based processes
problem.card.1.desc: Even today many clinical handoffs still rely on handwritten notes, printed sheets, and verbal summaries that are easy to misread, lose, or overlook between shifts.
problem.card.2.title: Fragmented information
problem.card.2.desc: In many places, patient data lives across disconnected tools such as spreadsheets, chat apps and partially filled forms that makes it difficult to get a complete, up-to-date clinical picture.
problem.card.3.title: Coordination delays
problem.card.3.desc: When medical teams cannot quickly access shared clinical records, critical decisions slow down and ultimately the patient is the one who suffers as a result.
problem.card.4.title: Loss of traceability
problem.card.4.desc: Without a centralized audit trail, it becomes nearly impossible to reconstruct what happened, when, and who was responsible. This is ultimately, a key gap for clinical accountability.
problem.card.5.title: Discontinuity of care
problem.card.5.desc: Shift changes are high-risk moments. When the outgoing team cannot fully pass context to the incoming team, patient care suffers from gaps in awareness and continuity.
problem.card.6.title: Hidden inefficiencies
problem.card.6.desc: Time spent searching for scattered records, re-entering data, or using slow/old services adds up which silently drains nursing capacity that should go directly to patients.
problem.callout: These are not isolated inconveniences but rather a pattern in many places. They are systemic risks embedded in how clinical teams communicate. With the well-being of many people at stake, these issues are too high to leave unaddressed.
benefits.lead: A single, clean flow for daily handoffs, clinical follow-up, and service oversight across the care team.
benefits.card.1.title: Less time searching, more time caring
benefits.card.1.desc: The medical staff spend less time searching for notes, messages or spreadsheets and more time caring for patients.
benefits.card.2.title: Shared context across shifts
benefits.card.2.desc: Everyone sees the same updated information, which helps clinicians stay aligned and avoid duplicated work.
benefits.card.3.title: A clear record of what happened
benefits.card.3.desc: Actions, changes, and crinical events stay organized for review, supervision, and accountability.
benefits.card.4.title: Reduce manual mistakes
benefits.card.4.desc: Centralized data lowers the risk of omissions, duplicate entries, and inconsistent handoff details.
benefits.card.5.title: Simple enough for busy teams
benefits.card.5.desc: An intuitive layout makes adoption easier for users who need to move fast without losing clarity.
benefits.summary.badge: Clinical clarity available 24 hours a day
benefits.summary.title: A single source of control
benefits.summary.text: A service focused on ensuring that clinical information is accurate, organised and available when needed.
benefits.metric.1.label: Faster handoffs
benefits.metric.2.label: Operational control
benefits.metric.3.label: Safer care
